modules.module_title: Introduction to Swimming instruction, Pool Hygiene & Water Chemistry
Swim Stroke Mechanics: Freestyle, Backstroke, Breaststroke & Butterfly
Lesson Planning, Teaching Methodologies & Learner Progression
Water Safety, Lifesaving Techniques & Pool Emergency Action Plans (EAP)
First Aid, CPR/AED for Drowning Victims & Spinal Injury Management
Professional Ethics, Legal Liability & Swim School Operations

questions.question: Swimming Pool Instructor ka primary objective kya hota hai?

options.A: Learners ko swimming strokes (freestyle, backstroke) seekhana, pool safety maintain karna aur active lifesaving support dena
options.B: Only running commercial juice counters next to pools
options.C: Writing computer programs for diving scores online
options.D: Zero student water safety supervision

questions.explanation: Swim instructor student mechanics refine karta hai, water panic reduce karta hai aur drowning cases prevent karne ke liye active lifeguard checks run karta hai.

questions.question: Pool water chemistry audits checks me 'Free Chlorine' level target range kya hoti hai?

options.A: 1.0 to 3.0 ppm (parts per million) to kill bacteria and pathogens safely
options.B: 10.0 to 20.0 ppm (excess limits that burn eyes)
options.C: Zero chlorine (pure water only)
options.D: Chlorine is not tested in swimming pools

questions.explanation: Free chlorine pathogen growth control karti hai. Standard range 1-3 ppm safe and sanitizing water environment define karti hai.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics me 'Freestyle' (Front Crawl) execution time body rotation guideline?

options.A: Rotate torso slightly (30-45 degrees) side-to-side along the long axis with every arm stroke
options.B: Keep hips and chest perfectly flat on water without rotation
options.C: Keep head rotated sideways continuously to look at ceiling
options.D: Zero body rotation is used

questions.explanation: Axial rotation drag reduce karta hai, shoulder joints safety confirm karta hai aur easy bilateral breathing allow karta hai.

questions.question: Active drowning victim response checks me 'Drowning Signs' kya characterize karte hain?

options.A: Vertical body position, arms flapping sideways as if pushing water down, zero progress, and inability to shout for help
options.B: Waving hands high and shouting 'Help me!' loudly multiple times
options.C: Floating peacefully on back with eyes closed
options.D: Drowning victims always swim back to stairs

questions.explanation: Drowning silent reaction hota hai. Vocal folds restrict ho jate hain aur victim water support leverage lene ke liye hands press up-down karta hai.

questions.question: Lifesaving rescue protocols checks me 'Reach or Throw, Don't Go' rule kya signify karta hai?

options.A: Attempt rescue using poles, ropes, or ring buoys first before entering water, to prevent double drowning danger
options.B: Jump into water immediately and grab victim neck from front
options.C: Tell the victim to walk out of pool himself
options.D: Zero rescue gear is used

questions.explanation: Panic state drowning victim rescuer ko drag down kar sakta hai. Isliye out-of-water assists (throw buoys) ko first priority di jati hai.

questions.question: Pool water sanitization checks me 'pH level' range standard recommended settings?

options.A: 7.2 to 7.8 (matches human tear pH to prevent eye irritation and verify chlorine effectiveness)
options.B: 5.0 to 6.0 (highly acidic water limits)
options.C: 9.0 to 10.0 (alkaline scales building)
options.D: pH values do not affect pool health

questions.explanation: pH 7.2-7.8 range comfort aur corrosion prevention confirm karti hai. Chlorine disinfection speed pH level par hyper dependent rehti hai.

questions.question: Swim stroke execution me 'Breaststroke' kick timing checks standard movement shape?

options.A: Whip Kick (knees bent, feet flexed outward, then sweep circular and snap together)
options.B: Flutter kick (up and down fast toe movement)
options.C: Dolphin kick (both legs undulating together like dolphin tail)
options.D: Breaststroke uses zero leg kick

questions.explanation: Whip kick circular rotation thrust generate karti hai breaststroke glide cycles me, while maintaining low drag profile.

questions.question: Pool safety emergency plan checks me 'EAP' ka full form kya hota hai?

options.A: Emergency Action Plan (clear step-by-step role delegation during accidents/rescue events)
options.B: Employee Attendance Program
options.C: Equipment Audit Procedure
options.D: Zero planning utility

questions.explanation: EAP specifies who calls emergency numbers, who performs CPR, and who manages exit gates during accident runs.

questions.question: Drowning victim first aid CPR checks checks me, chest compressions to rescue breaths ratio standard?

options.A: 30 compressions followed by 2 rescue breaths (30:2 ratio)
options.B: 15 compressions to 5 breaths
options.C: 100 compressions only with zero breaths
options.D: CPR does not involve chest press steps

questions.explanation: 30:2 CPR protocol global medical standards checks (AHA/Red Cross) ke under oxygenation cycle restore karta hai.

questions.question: Swim instructor professional ethics checks me child safety policies guidelines?

options.A: Never supervise children groups alone in closed locker spaces, and always verify parent presence during exits
options.B: Leave children in pool deep zones unattended to build self confidence
options.C: Train kids in stormy lightning weather conditions
options.D: Zero ethics standards apply

questions.explanation: Child safety and abuse prevention regulations strict supervision guidelines outline karti hain to protect kid safety.

questions.question: Swimming strokes 'Backstroke' body alignment guidelines?

options.A: Keep hips high, head back, chin slightly tucked, looking straight up (prevents water entering nose)
options.B: Sag hips low to sit in water
options.C: Keep head raised forward to look at toes
options.D: Backstroke body positioning is completely random

questions.explanation: Hips high and head back keeps body streamlined on surface, reducing drag and making breathing easy.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Butterfly stroke' what kick style is used?

options.A: Dolphin kick (legs move together in whip undulating pattern driven by hips)
options.B: Flutter kick (alternating feet)
options.C: Whip kick (circular sweep)
options.D: Butterfly uses zero leg kicks

questions.explanation: Dolphin kick relies on core and hip undulation, driving the butterfly body lift cycle.

questions.question: Pool safety equipment 'Rescue Tube' why is it red/orange color foam?

options.A: High visibility colors; foam offers buoyancy support to keep victim and rescuer afloat
options.B: Required by chemical dye laws
options.C: Foam absorbs chlorine smell
options.D: Color has zero visibility functions

questions.explanation: Bright colors ensure visibility. The buoyant foam tube supports multiple victims in water.

questions.question: First aid spinal injury handling 'Spinal immobilization in water' what technique is used?

options.A: In-line stabilization (head/neck splinted between rescuer forearms; align body until backboard arrives)
options.B: Pull victim out of pool quickly by hands
options.C: Roll victim on side to drain water
options.D: Spinal injuries do not require alignment checks

questions.explanation: Spinal injury requires immobilizing head/neck (in-line stabilization) in water to prevent paralysis risk.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Liability waiver logs' why are they signed before lessons start?

options.A: Document safety terms, verify client health disclosures, and protect school from legal disputes
options.B: Required by Google AdSense algorithms
options.C: Calculates daily water consumption taxes
options.D: Waivers have zero legal utility

questions.explanation: Liability waivers document risks, verifying client consent and protecting facilities from legal claims.

options.A: Whip Kick (knees bent, feet flexed outward, then sweep circular and snap together)
options.B: Flutter kick (up and down fast toe movement)
options.C: Dolphin kick (both legs undulating together like dolphin tail)
options.D: Breaststroke uses zero leg kick

questions.explanation: Whip kick circular rotation thrust generate karti hai breaststroke glide cycles me, while maintaining low drag profile.

options.C: Keep head raised forward to look at toes
options.D: Backstroke body positioning is completely random

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Freestyle arm stroke phase' what is correct sequence?

options.A: Entry, Catch, Pull, Push, Recovery (5-phase arm cycle pattern)
options.B: Push, Pull, Slip, Relax
options.C: Swing, Lift, Drop, Pull
options.D: Arm strokes do not follow sequences

questions.explanation: The 5-phase freestyle arm cycle maximizes thrust while keeping resistance low in water.

questions.question: Pool water chemistry 'Combined Chlorine' what does high level indicate?

options.A: Presence of chloramines (causes strong chlorine smell and eye irritation; indicates dirty water)
options.B: High water clarity and sanitation levels
options.C: Too much free chlorine in pool
options.D: Chloramines do not affect water quality

questions.explanation: Chloramines cause eye burn and a strong chemical smell, signaling a need for shock treatment.

questions.question: Water safety rescue 'Active victim rescue' what approach is safest for swimming rescues?

options.A: Approach from behind, place rescue tube under victim's arms, and keep a safe distance between your faces
options.B: Approach from front, letting victim wrap arms around your neck
options.C: Pull victim down to floor to stop struggles
options.D: Approach direction has zero safety impact

questions.explanation: Approaching from behind prevents the panicked victim from grabbing you, keeping the rescuer safe.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'Water inhalation' why are rescue breaths given first in water rescues?

options.A: Drowning is a primary hypoxic event (oxygen deprivation); rapid breaths restore oxygen levels before chest compressions
options.B: Breaths help drain water out of stomach
options.C: Compressions do not work in water
options.D: Rescue breaths are not given to drowning victims

questions.explanation: Drowning depletes oxygen. Quick rescue breaths restore oxygenation, preventing cardiac arrest.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Lifeguard-to-swimmer ratios' what is standard ratio for beginner classes?

options.A: 1 lifeguard/instructor to maximum 10-12 beginner students
options.B: 1 to 50 students
options.C: Ratios are completely unregulated
options.D: Beginners do not require supervision ratios

questions.explanation: A 1:10-12 ratio ensures the instructor can monitor all beginners, keeping water activities safe.

questions.question: Freestyle arm stroke phase entry catch?

options.A: Entry, Catch, Pull, Push, Recovery (5-phase arm cycle pattern)
options.B: Push, Pull, Slip, Relax
options.C: Swing, Lift, Drop, Pull
options.D: Arm strokes do not follow sequences

questions.explanation: The 5-phase freestyle arm cycle maximizes thrust while keeping resistance low in water.

options.A: Presence of chloramines (causes strong chlorine smell and eye irritation; indicates dirty water)
options.B: High water clarity and sanitation levels
options.C: Too much free chlorine in pool
options.D: Chloramines do not affect water quality

questions.explanation: Chloramines cause eye burn and a strong chemical smell, signaling a need for shock treatment.

questions.question: Active victim approach behind rescue tube?

options.A: Approach from behind, place rescue tube under victim's arms, and keep a safe distance between your faces
options.B: Approach from front, letting victim wrap arms around your neck
options.C: Pull victim down to floor to stop struggles
options.D: Approach direction has zero safety impact

questions.explanation: Approaching from behind prevents the panicked victim from grabbing you, keeping the rescuer safe.

options.B: Breaths help drain water out of stomach
options.C: Compressions do not work in water
options.D: Rescue breaths are not given to drowning victims

questions.explanation: Drowning depletes oxygen. Quick rescue breaths restore oxygenation, preventing cardiac arrest.

questions.question: Beginner ratio 1 to 10 lifeguard?

options.A: 1 lifeguard/instructor to maximum 10-12 beginner students
options.B: 1 to 50 students
options.C: Ratios are completely unregulated
options.D: Beginners do not require supervision ratios

questions.explanation: A 1:10-12 ratio ensures the instructor can monitor all beginners, keeping water activities safe.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Freestyle kick' what kick style is used?

options.A: Flutter kick (alternating leg kicks starting from hips, ankles relaxed)
options.B: Whip kick (circular motion)
options.C: Dolphin kick
options.D: Freestyle uses zero leg kicks

questions.explanation: Flutter kick starts from the hips with relaxed ankles, helping keep the body streamlined.

questions.question: Pool water chemistry 'Total Dissolved Solids' (TDS) what indicates high TDS?

options.A: High accumulation of body sweat, skin oils, sunscreen, and pool chemicals (requires water dilution)
options.B: High water clarity scores
options.C: High chlorine sanitation levels
options.D: TDS does not affect water safety

questions.explanation: High TDS dulls water and reduces chlorine sanitizing power. Diluting the water is the standard fix.

questions.question: Water safety 'Passive victim rescue' what is first action step?

options.A: Turn victim face-up in water immediately, place rescue tube under back, and swim them to pool edge
options.B: Push victim down to pool floor
options.C: Wait at pool side for victim to float up
options.D: Passive victims do not require water entries

questions.explanation: Getting a passive victim's face out of the water immediately is vital to prevent brain damage.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'Foam in airway' how should rescuer handle white foam from victim mouth?

options.A: Wipe foam away and continue rescue breaths (do not try to drain or pump foam out of lungs)
options.B: Stop CPR immediately to drain foam
options.C: Perform abdominal thrusts to clear foam
options.D: Foam indicates death, stop rescue efforts

questions.explanation: Foam occurs when water mixes with air surfactant. Wipe it away and continue rescue breaths.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Water temperature standards' what is recommended range for baby classes?

options.A: 84 to 88 degrees Fahrenheit (29 to 31 degrees Celsius) to prevent hypothermia in infants
options.B: 60 degrees Fahrenheit
options.C: 100 degrees Fahrenheit
options.D: Water temperature does not affect babies

questions.explanation: Babies lose heat quickly. Warmer pool water (84-88°F) keeps infants comfortable and safe.

questions.question: Freestyle flutter kick hip ankles?

options.A: Flutter kick (alternating leg kicks starting from hips, ankles relaxed)
options.B: Whip kick (circular motion)
options.C: Dolphin kick
options.D: Freestyle uses zero leg kicks

questions.explanation: Flutter kick starts from the hips with relaxed ankles, helping keep the body streamlined.

questions.question: TDS Total Dissolved Solids sweat chemical?

options.A: High accumulation of body sweat, skin oils, sunscreen, and pool chemicals (requires water dilution)
options.B: High water clarity scores
options.C: High chlorine sanitation levels
options.D: TDS does not affect water safety

questions.explanation: High TDS dulls water and reduces chlorine sanitizing power. Diluting the water is the standard fix.

questions.question: Passive victim face up back support?

options.A: Turn victim face-up in water immediately, place rescue tube under back, and swim them to pool edge
options.B: Push victim down to pool floor
options.C: Wait at pool side for victim to float up
options.D: Passive victims do not require water entries

questions.explanation: Getting a passive victim's face out of the water immediately is vital to prevent brain damage.

questions.question: Drowning foam mouth wipe away?

options.A: Wipe foam away and continue rescue breaths (do not try to drain or pump foam out of lungs)
options.B: Stop CPR immediately to drain foam
options.C: Perform abdominal thrusts to clear foam
options.D: Foam indicates death, stop rescue efforts

questions.explanation: Foam occurs when water mixes with air surfactant. Wipe it away and continue rescue breaths.

questions.question: Baby swim class water temperature 88?

options.A: 84 to 88 degrees Fahrenheit (29 to 31 degrees Celsius) to prevent hypothermia in infants
options.B: 60 degrees Fahrenheit
options.C: 100 degrees Fahrenheit
options.D: Water temperature does not affect babies

questions.explanation: Babies lose heat quickly. Warmer pool water (84-88°F) keeps infants comfortable and safe.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Backstroke arm rotation' what is correct entry point?

options.A: Pinky finger enters water first, arm straight next to ear (reduces drag and prepares catch)
options.B: Thumb enters water first
options.C: Flat palm slap entry
options.D: Arm rotation does not affect backstroke

questions.explanation: Pinky-first entry puts the hand in catch position immediately, preventing shoulder strain.

questions.question: Pool sanitation chemistry 'Superchlorination' (Shocking) why is it performed?

options.A: Raising chlorine levels aggressively to break down chloramines and kill resistant algae/pathogens
options.B: To make water smell highly of chlorine
options.C: Reduces pH levels to neutral
options.D: Shocking is not used in pool operations

questions.explanation: Shocking breaks down irritating chloramines and kills resistant algae, restoring water sanitation.

questions.question: Water safety 'Cervical spine injury in deep pool' how should victim be stabilized?

options.A: Use spinal backboard in water, secure straps, and slide up stairs with head immobilizer blocks
options.B: Lift victim over shoulders and carry out
options.C: Encourage victim to swim to shallow zone
options.D: Spinal backboards are not used in water

questions.explanation: Backboarding in water stabilizes the spine, avoiding secondary injury during extrication.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'AED on wet surfaces' what safety check is run?

options.A: Wipe victim chest dry, ensure chest area is not in standing puddle of water (rescuer must be on dry area)
options.B: Apply pads directly on wet soaking chest
options.C: Pads can be applied underwater
options.D: AED cannot be used on drowning victims

questions.explanation: Wiping the chest dry prevents the shock current from dispersing across wet skin, ensuring it hits the heart.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Swim levels progression' what is first skill taught to fresh beginners?

options.A: Water acclimatization, face submersion, and bubble blowing (getting comfortable with breathing in water)
options.B: Deep water diving drills
options.C: Learning butterfly stroke arms
options.D: Beginners start with tread water drills

questions.explanation: Water comfort, face dips, and bubble blowing prevent panic, building a base for stroke mechanics.

questions.question: Backstroke pinky finger water entry?

options.A: Pinky finger enters water first, arm straight next to ear (reduces drag and prepares catch)
options.B: Thumb enters water first
options.C: Flat palm slap entry
options.D: Arm rotation does not affect backstroke

questions.explanation: Pinky-first entry puts the hand in catch position immediately, preventing shoulder strain.

questions.question: Shocking pool superchlorination chloramines?

options.A: Raising chlorine levels aggressively to break down chloramines and kill resistant algae/pathogens
options.B: To make water smell highly of chlorine
options.C: Reduces pH levels to neutral
options.D: Shocking is not used in pool operations

questions.explanation: Shocking breaks down irritating chloramines and kills resistant algae, restoring water sanitation.

questions.question: Spinal backboard water stabilization blocks?

options.A: Use spinal backboard in water, secure straps, and slide up stairs with head immobilizer blocks
options.B: Lift victim over shoulders and carry out
options.C: Encourage victim to swim to shallow zone
options.D: Spinal backboards are not used in water

questions.explanation: Backboarding in water stabilizes the spine, avoiding secondary injury during extrication.

questions.question: AED chest dry wet puddle check?

options.A: Wipe victim chest dry, ensure chest area is not in standing puddle of water (rescuer must be on dry area)
options.B: Apply pads directly on wet soaking chest
options.C: Pads can be applied underwater
options.D: AED cannot be used on drowning victims

questions.explanation: Wiping the chest dry prevents the shock current from dispersing across wet skin, ensuring it hits the heart.

questions.question: Beginner water acclimatization bubble blow?

options.A: Water acclimatization, face submersion, and bubble blowing (getting comfortable with breathing in water)
options.B: Deep water diving drills
options.C: Learning butterfly stroke arms
options.D: Beginners start with tread water drills

questions.explanation: Water comfort, face dips, and bubble blowing prevent panic, building a base for stroke mechanics.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Freestyle breathing' what is bilateral breathing?

options.A: Breathing to alternate sides (eg breathing every 3 strokes, alternating left and right)
options.B: Breathing with mouth and nose simultaneously
options.C: Inhaling underwater using snorkel gear
options.D: Bilateral breathing is not used in freestyle

questions.explanation: Bilateral breathing balances shoulder development and keeps swim lines straight in the lane.

questions.question: Pool sanitation chemistry 'Fecal accident protocol' what is first action step?

options.A: Evacuate pool immediately, close facility, remove waste, and superchlorinate pool water
options.B: Ignore and let filtration systems clear waste
options.C: Pour bleach directly onto waste while swimmers are in pool
options.D: No protocols exist for fecal accidents

questions.explanation: Evacuating and shocking the pool is vital to destroy pathogens like Cryptosporidium, protecting swimmers.

questions.question: Water safety 'Active drowning victim struggle duration' how long does a victim struggle before sinking?

options.A: 20 to 60 seconds (requires immediate lifeguard response)
options.B: 10 minutes
options.C: 2 hours
options.D: Victims can float indefinitely during struggle

questions.explanation: Panicked victims sink within 20-60 seconds, meaning lifeguards must act immediately to prevent drowning.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'Rescue breaths on unresponsive victim' what is first action if chest does not rise?

options.A: Re-tilt the head, check seal, and attempt second breath (ensure airway is open)
options.B: Perform abdominal thrusts immediately
options.C: Stop CPR and wait for paramedics
options.D: Skip breaths and do compressions only

questions.explanation: If the chest doesn't rise, re-tilting the head clears airway blocks, ensuring air enters the lungs.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Child protection standards' what is the rule regarding taking photos?

options.A: Strictly prohibit photography unless explicit written consent is obtained from parents
options.B: Allow taking photos of all children for advertising
options.C: Let instructors post photos on personal blogs
options.D: No photography rules exist in swim schools

questions.explanation: Securing parent consent before taking photos protects child privacy and avoids legal violations.

questions.question: Freestyle bilateral breathing side alternate?

options.A: Breathing to alternate sides (eg breathing every 3 strokes, alternating left and right)
options.B: Breathing with mouth and nose simultaneously
options.C: Inhaling underwater using snorkel gear
options.D: Bilateral breathing is not used in freestyle

questions.explanation: Bilateral breathing balances shoulder development and keeps swim lines straight in the lane.

questions.question: Fecal accident evacuate pool superchlorinate?

options.A: Evacuate pool immediately, close facility, remove waste, and superchlorinate pool water
options.B: Ignore and let filtration systems clear waste
options.C: Pour bleach directly onto waste while swimmers are in pool
options.D: No protocols exist for fecal accidents

questions.explanation: Evacuating and shocking the pool is vital to destroy pathogens like Cryptosporidium, protecting swimmers.

questions.question: Drowning struggle duration 60 seconds?

options.A: 20 to 60 seconds (requires immediate lifeguard response)
options.B: 10 minutes
options.C: 2 hours
options.D: Victims can float indefinitely during struggle

questions.explanation: Panicked victims sink within 20-60 seconds, meaning lifeguards must act immediately to prevent drowning.

questions.question: Rescue breaths fail re tilt head seal?

options.A: Re-tilt the head, check seal, and attempt second breath (ensure airway is open)
options.B: Perform abdominal thrusts immediately
options.C: Stop CPR and wait for paramedics
options.D: Skip breaths and do compressions only

questions.explanation: If the chest doesn't rise, re-tilting the head clears airway blocks, ensuring air enters the lungs.

questions.question: Photo parental consent child protection?

options.A: Strictly prohibit photography unless explicit written consent is obtained from parents
options.B: Allow taking photos of all children for advertising
options.C: Let instructors post photos on personal blogs
options.D: No photography rules exist in swim schools

questions.explanation: Securing parent consent before taking photos protects child privacy and avoids legal violations.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Breaststroke breathing' when does the breath occur in the stroke cycle?

options.A: During the arm pull phase (shoulders rise, head lifts naturally to inhale)
options.B: During the glide phase
options.C: During the leg kick phase
options.D: Breaststroke breathing occurs underwater

questions.explanation: Inhaling as the shoulders rise during the arm pull ensures breathing fits the stroke rhythm.

questions.question: Pool safety equipment 'Spineboard straps' what is the sequence of securing straps on a victim?

options.A: Chest first, then hips, then legs, and head immobilizer last (stabilize torso movement first)
options.B: Head first, then legs, then chest
options.C: Legs first, then chest, then head
options.D: Strap sequence does not matter

questions.explanation: Securing the chest and torso first prevents secondary spinal movement during head splinting.

questions.question: Lifesaving techniques 'Self-rescue in currents' how should swimmer float if swept in river currents?

options.A: Float on back with feet pointing downstream (feet absorb impact with rocks, protecting head)
options.B: Swim against current to return to start point
options.C: Dive to bottom and hold onto floor stones
options.D: Curl into ball shape to float

questions.explanation: Floating feet-first protects your head and spine from impacts with rocks in fast currents.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'Dry drowning vs Wet drowning' what is dry drowning mechanism?

options.A: Laryngospasm (water contact causes vocal cords to spasm and close, blocking airway without water in lungs)
options.B: Airway blocked by food items during swim
options.C: Cardiac arrest caused by cold shock
options.D: Dry drowning is not a medical term

questions.explanation: Water drops on vocal cords can trigger laryngospasm, sealing the airway and causing hypoxia without water entering the lungs.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Incident reports logs' how long must logs be archived legally?

options.A: Min 3 to 7 years depending on local statute of limitations for injury claims
options.B: 1 month only
options.C: Logs are discarded immediately after shift ends
options.D: Incident reports do not require archiving

questions.explanation: Archiving logs protects the school, providing evidence if injury claims are filed later.

questions.question: Breaststroke arm pull breathing lift?

options.A: During the arm pull phase (shoulders rise, head lifts naturally to inhale)
options.B: During the glide phase
options.C: During the leg kick phase
options.D: Breaststroke breathing occurs underwater

questions.explanation: Inhaling as the shoulders rise during the arm pull ensures breathing fits the stroke rhythm.

questions.question: Spineboard strap chest first head last?

options.B: Head first, then legs, then chest
options.C: Legs first, then chest, then head
options.D: Strap sequence does not matter

questions.explanation: Securing the chest and torso first prevents secondary spinal movement during head splinting.

questions.question: Self rescue current feet downstream back?

options.A: Float on back with feet pointing downstream (feet absorb impact with rocks, protecting head)
options.B: Swim against current to return to start point
options.C: Dive to bottom and hold onto floor stones
options.D: Curl into ball shape to float

questions.explanation: Floating feet-first protects your head and spine from impacts with rocks in fast currents.

options.A: Laryngospasm (water contact causes vocal cords to spasm and close, blocking airway without water in lungs)
options.B: Airway blocked by food items during swim
options.C: Cardiac arrest caused by cold shock
options.D: Dry drowning is not a medical term

questions.explanation: Water drops on vocal cords can trigger laryngospasm, sealing the airway and causing hypoxia without water entering the lungs.

questions.question: Incident report archiving 7 years?

options.A: Min 3 to 7 years depending on local statute of limitations for injury claims
options.B: 1 month only
options.C: Logs are discarded immediately after shift ends
options.D: Incident reports do not require archiving

questions.explanation: Archiving logs protects the school, providing evidence if injury claims are filed later.

questions.question: Swim stroke mechanics 'Freestyle body position' how is drag minimized?

options.A: Keep body horizontal and close to surface, head aligned, eyes looking down at pool floor
options.B: Sag hips down to swim at angle
options.C: Keep head raised above water always
options.D: Body position does not affect drag in water

questions.explanation: Looking down aligns the neck and spine, keeping hips high and reducing drag.

questions.question: Pool water sanitation 'Shocking chlorine levels' when is pool safe to re-enter?

options.A: When free chlorine levels fall back below 5.0 ppm (inspect with test kit)
options.B: Immediately after adding shock chemicals
options.C: When water smells strongly of chlorine
options.D: Safe levels are not tested after shocking

questions.explanation: High chlorine (above 5.0 ppm) can irritate skin and eyes. Ensure levels return to safe ranges before swimmers enter.

questions.question: Water safety 'Active vs Passive drowning victim rescue' when is a non-contact rescue possible?

options.A: When victim is conscious, responsive, and close enough to grab a thrown ring buoy or pole
options.B: When victim is unconscious on pool floor
options.C: Non-contact rescues are never possible
options.D: Only when the victim is a skilled swimmer

questions.explanation: Non-contact rescues (eg poles, buoys) eliminate the risk of a panicked victim grabbing the rescuer.

questions.question: First aid drowning 'AED pads dry chest' why is chest drying mandatory?

options.A: Prevents electrical current from flowing across wet skin surface rather than through heart
options.B: Required by chemical dye codes
options.C: Drying chest makes pads look clean
options.D: AED pads do not require dry chest

questions.explanation: Wiping the chest dry ensures the electrical shock flows directly through the heart muscle.

questions.question: Swim school operations 'Parent-child class format' what is the parent's role?

options.A: Provide direct physical support and reassurance in water under instructor guidance
options.B: Sit on pool side chairs and watch instructor only
options.C: Instruct other children in pool
options.D: Parents have zero role in water

questions.explanation: Having parents in the pool provides physical support and comfort for infants, helping them acclimate.

questions.question: Horizontal body position freestyle floor?

options.A: Keep body horizontal and close to surface, head aligned, eyes looking down at pool floor
options.B: Sag hips down to swim at angle
options.C: Keep head raised above water always
options.D: Body position does not affect drag in water

questions.explanation: Looking down aligns the neck and spine, keeping hips high and reducing drag.

options.A: When free chlorine levels fall back below 5.0 ppm (inspect with test kit)
options.B: Immediately after adding shock chemicals
options.C: When water smells strongly of chlorine
options.D: Safe levels are not tested after shocking

questions.explanation: High chlorine (above 5.0 ppm) can irritate skin and eyes. Ensure levels return to safe ranges before swimmers enter.

options.A: When victim is conscious, responsive, and close enough to grab a thrown ring buoy or pole
options.B: When victim is unconscious on pool floor
options.C: Non-contact rescues are never possible
options.D: Only when the victim is a skilled swimmer

questions.explanation: Non-contact rescues (eg poles, buoys) eliminate the risk of a panicked victim grabbing the rescuer.

options.A: Prevents electrical current from flowing across wet skin surface rather than through heart
options.B: Required by chemical dye codes
options.C: Drying chest makes pads look clean
options.D: AED pads do not require dry chest

questions.explanation: Wiping the chest dry ensures the electrical shock flows directly through the heart muscle.

options.A: Provide direct physical support and reassurance in water under instructor guidance
options.B: Sit on pool side chairs and watch instructor only
options.C: Instruct other children in pool
options.D: Parents have zero role in water

questions.explanation: Having parents in the pool provides physical support and comfort for infants, helping them acclimate.

questions.question: Swimming Pool Instructor final launch audits why water rescue simulation is key final step?

options.A: Verifies instructor ability to spot drowning signs, execute reach/throw rescues and perform CPR/AED
options.B: Video editing file sizes compression
options.C: Subtitles translate tools automatic
options.D: Zero utility coordinates check

questions.explanation: A simulated rescue test ensures the instructor can handle emergencies and perform CPR under pressure.

questions.question: Swimming Pool Instructor core goal?

options.A: Learners ko swimming strokes (freestyle, backstroke) seekhana, pool safety maintain karna aur active lifesaving support dena
options.B: Only running commercial juice counters next to pools
options.C: Writing computer programs for diving scores online
options.D: Zero student water safety supervision

questions.explanation: Swim instructor student mechanics refine karta hai, water panic reduce karta hai aur drowning cases prevent karne ke liye active lifeguard checks run karta hai.

questions.question: Chlorine level swimming pool disinfection?

options.A: 1.0 to 3.0 ppm (parts per million) to kill bacteria and pathogens safely
options.B: 10.0 to 20.0 ppm (excess limits that burn eyes)
options.C: Zero chlorine (pure water only)
options.D: Chlorine is not tested in swimming pools

questions.explanation: Free chlorine pathogen growth control karti hai. Standard range 1-3 ppm safe and sanitizing water environment define karti hai.

questions.question: Front crawl body rotation long axis?

options.A: Rotate torso slightly (30-45 degrees) side-to-side along the long axis with every arm stroke
options.B: Keep hips and chest perfectly flat on water without rotation
options.C: Keep head rotated sideways continuously to look at ceiling
options.D: Zero body rotation is used

questions.explanation: Axial rotation drag reduce karta hai, shoulder joints safety confirm karta hai aur easy bilateral breathing allow karta hai.

questions.question: Drowning symptoms silent reaction vertical?

options.A: Vertical body position, arms flapping sideways as if pushing water down, zero progress, and inability to shout for help
options.B: Waving hands high and shouting 'Help me!' loudly multiple times
options.C: Floating peacefully on back with eyes closed
options.D: Drowning victims always swim back to stairs

questions.explanation: Drowning silent reaction hota hai. Vocal folds restrict ho jate hain aur victim water support leverage lene ke liye hands press up-down karta hai.

questions.question: Reach throw don't go double drowning?

options.A: Attempt rescue using poles, ropes, or ring buoys first before entering water, to prevent double drowning danger
options.B: Jump into water immediately and grab victim neck from front
options.C: Tell the victim to walk out of pool himself
options.D: Zero rescue gear is used

questions.explanation: Panic state drowning victim rescuer ko drag down kar sakta hai. Isliye out-of-water assists (throw buoys) ko first priority di jati hai.

questions.question: Swimming pool pH values comfort limits?

options.A: 7.2 to 7.8 (matches human tear pH to prevent eye irritation and verify chlorine effectiveness)
options.B: 5.0 to 6.0 (highly acidic water limits)
options.C: 9.0 to 10.0 (alkaline scales building)
options.D: pH values do not affect pool health

questions.explanation: pH 7.2-7.8 range comfort aur corrosion prevention confirm karti hai. Chlorine disinfection speed pH level par hyper dependent rehti hai.

questions.question: Breaststroke whip kick circular path?

options.A: Whip Kick (knees bent, feet flexed outward, then sweep circular and snap together)
options.B: Flutter kick (up and down fast toe movement)
options.C: Dolphin kick (both legs undulating together like dolphin tail)
options.D: Breaststroke uses zero leg kick

questions.explanation: Whip kick circular rotation thrust generate karti hai breaststroke glide cycles me, while maintaining low drag profile.

questions.question: Emergency Action Plan role delegation?

options.A: Emergency Action Plan (clear step-by-step role delegation during accidents/rescue events)
options.B: Employee Attendance Program
options.C: Equipment Audit Procedure
options.D: Zero planning utility

questions.explanation: EAP specifies who calls emergency numbers, who performs CPR, and who manages exit gates during accident runs.

questions.question: Drowning CPR chest compressions breaths ratio?

options.A: 30 compressions followed by 2 rescue breaths (30:2 ratio)
options.B: 15 compressions to 5 breaths
options.C: 100 compressions only with zero breaths
options.D: CPR does not involve chest press steps

questions.explanation: 30:2 CPR protocol global medical standards checks (AHA/Red Cross) ke under oxygenation cycle restore karta hai.
